refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral, specifying 'communication quality' without prescribing how to achieve it. The FR is atomic as it addresses one clear functional requirement related to teleoperation communication performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit (e.g., ≤ 1%).

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters to meet packet loss requirements
Incorporate real-time network monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters to control packet loss
Use buffering and smoothing techniques at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on network conditions and error correction algorithms
Interaction between retransmission logic and teleoperation control timing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates the communication quality control within the protocol layer, supporting independence from other system functions. Adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms can be designed modularly to meet packet loss targets without tightly coupling to other system components.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters and do not degrade unexpectedly
Risk that retransmission delays may impact teleoperation responsiveness if not carefully managed
Assumes error correction algorithms can be tuned to meet the ≤1% packet loss target under operational policies
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time network condition monitoring as a supporting DP to enhance adaptability and robustness of the communication quality control.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core communication and networking functionalities, including protocol implementation and adaptive error correction mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending and receiving data packets over the network, essential for implementing communication protocols.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs status and performance data related to communication quality and error correction effectiveness.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the communication protocol logically belongs here; minimal diffusion since error correction and retransmission are core networking functions.
Vehicle module may use communication but implementing protocol here would cause significant diffusion and coupling with other modules.
Teleoperation depends on communication but embedding protocol here risks duplication and diffusion.
Safety Services may monitor communication but implementing protocol here is not cohesive and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol, as it centralizes networking functions and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not explicitly defined here may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If adaptive error correction requires real-time feedback from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, some interface complexity may arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying an adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission to maintain packet loss within customer-defined limits, fulfilling the teleoperation communication quality requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces not explicitly defined may cause coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If adaptive error correction requires real-time feedback from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, interface complexity and diffusion risk may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary, minimizing diffusion risk and supporting maintainability.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface, which outputs communication quality and error correction performance data, enabling validation against the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and logical, as this module encompasses the core communication functionalities required to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define interfaces and dependencies with lower-level hardware modules to reduce coupling risks.
Clarify and document any required real-time feedback mechanisms from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules to NGHE Solution to manage interface complexity.
Consider adding monitoring or safety checks within Safety Services to ensure operational policies are met without embedding protocol logic there.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a well-contained design parameter and implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the DP directly fulfills the FR. Although some interface and coupling risks are noted, these are manageable through recommended revisions rather than further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and contained design is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: To explicitly define interfaces and dependencies with lower-level hardware modules to reduce coupling risks, clarify real-time feedback mechanisms from Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, and consider adding monitoring or safety checks within Safety Services to ensure operational policies without embedding protocol logic there.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the adaptive communication protocol effectively.
Verification via telemetry monitoring is sufficient to validate communication quality.
Interface complexity can be managed through documentation and design controls.
open_risks: Potential dependency on lower-level hardware interfaces causing coupling outside NGHE Solution.
Interface complexity and diffusion risk from real-time feedback requirements.
Ensuring operational policies are enforced without embedding protocol logic in Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications with lower-level hardware modules.
Documentation of real-time feedback mechanisms and their impact on interface complexity.
Verification test results demonstrating packet loss ≤1%.
